content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES Corv1r1IsSIoN
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
• Sacramento. CA 958)2-0807
(9)6) 322-5660
. Fax (916) 322-0886
April 22, 1999
David D’Arco
Staff Counsel
Health and Welfare Data Center
1651 Alliambra Boulevard
Sacramento, California 95816
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-99-082
Dear Mr. D’Arco:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ You are submitting this request for advice on behalf of Mr.
Russell Bohart. On April 13, 1999, this office received a letter from Mr. Bohart stating that he
has authorized the Health and Welfare Data Center to be his representative for the purpose of
seeking our advice.
Due to the general nature ofthe questions you have posed and the corresponding lack of
particular facts regarding Mr. Bohart’s past, present and future employment activities, we are
declining to provide either formal advice or informal assistance.
2 However, we are including for
your and Mr. Bohart’s use and reference a copy of the Commission’s fact sheet on post
The Act is codified in Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title
2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations. We also note at this juncture that the original
requestor for this letter was Harold Ferber, Chief Counsel for the Health and Welfare Data Center (“HWDC”).
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of April 7, 1999, you informed me that Mr. Ferber is no longer with HWDC
and that further correspondence regarding this matter should be directed to you. Accordingly, this response is
addressed as you instructed.
2 You have not explained for us the nature of Mr. Bohart’s prior (or current) duties, the identity of the
private companies involved in decisions made, participated in or influenced by Mr. Bohart while he was the director
of the HWDC, nor the beginning and ending dates of Mr. Bohart’s vacation status. We also do not know if Mr.
Bohart has secured new employment or, if he is still searching, the identities of the companies he is soliciting (or
being solicited by) and the extent of his former and present interaction with prospective employers.


File No. G-99-082
Page 2
governmental employment together with copies of newly adopted Regulations 18741.1, 18746.1,
18746.2 and 18747. Please also note that the Commission will be considering shortly an
amended fact sheet on this subject. We expect the Commission to approve the fact sheet and its
public distribution in the near future.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
LO&L&1
By:
Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:LLD:tls
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: What are the Political Reform Act implications for Mr. Russell Bohart regarding post-governmental employment restrictions and his activities as former director of the Health and Welfare Data Center?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide formal advice or informal assistance due to the general nature of the questions and lack of particular facts regarding Mr. Bohart's past, present, and future employment activities, but provides relevant factsheets and regulations as reference materials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter rather than an advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist; the question and conclusion are implicit in the content. The letter references lack of key facts as the reason for declining. Structured as an advisory response but with a negative determination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the Political Reform Act implications for Mr. Russell Bohart regarding post-governmental employment restrictions and his activities as former director of the Health and Welfare Data Center?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide formal advice or informal assistance due to the general nature of the questions and lack of particular facts regarding Mr. Bohart's past, present, and future employment activities, but provides relevant factsheets and regulations as reference materials.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide formal or informal advice to Mr. Russell Bohart regarding Political Reform Act compliance for post-governmental employment due to insufficient factual specificity, while providing reference materials on the subject.
